solution: 
```
class ExamRoom:

    def __init__(self, n: int):
        self.students = set()
        self.last_seat = -1

    def seat(self) -> int:
        if not self.students:
            self.students.add(0)
            return 0
        max_dist = 0
        seat = 0
        for i in sorted(self.students):
            if i > self.last_seat:
                dist = i - self.last_seat
                if dist > max_dist:
                    max_dist = dist
                    seat = self.last_seat + (dist + 1) // 2
            self.last_seat = i
        self.students.add(seat)
        return seat

    def leave(self, p: int) -> None:
        self.students.remove(p)
        if p > 0 and p - 1 in self.students:
            self.students.remove(p - 1)
            self.students.add((p - 1 + p) // 2)
        elif p < len(self.students) - 1 and p + 1 in self.students:
            self.students.remove(p + 1)
            self.students.add((p + p + 1) // 2)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a set to store the seats occupied by students. The `seat` method finds the seat with the maximum distance to the closest student and adds it to the set. The `leave` method removes the student from the set and adjusts the set accordingly to maintain the maximum distance. The time complexity is O(log n) due to the sorting operation in the `seat` method.
